briefdescription.para: Array representing the transitions table of the FSM RGB light.
Apply correction to an RGB color based on the intensity value.

detaileddescription.para: This function takes a regular color and applies a reduction based on the given intensity.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. For each channel of the RGB color, scale its value by the intensity given. Use float numbers to operate. ✅ 2. Save it in the color struct as an integer. To round it properly add 0.5 to the result. This 0.5 is used to round the floating-point result to the nearest integer instead of truncating, improving pwm accuracy especially at low brightness levels. Finally, cast it to uint8_t and overwrite the results into the color.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the color to be corrected.
Linear input intensity, in the range [0, 100].

briefdescription.para: Check if a new color has to be set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_rgb_light_t.

simplesect.para: true If a new color has to be set
false If a new color does not have to be set

briefdescription.para: Check if the RGB light is set to be active (ON), independently if it is idle or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_rgb_light_t.

simplesect.para: true If the RGB light system has been indicated to be active independently if it is idle or not.
false If the RGB light system has been indicated to be inactive.

briefdescription.para: Check if the RGB light is set to be inactive (OFF).

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Return the inverse of the flag status

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_rgb_light_t.

simplesect.para: true If the RGB light system has been indicated to be inactive.
false If the RGB light system has been indicated to be active.

briefdescription.para: Turn the RGB light system ON for the first time.

simplesect.para: In this version the RGB light starts ON with no color. This is: the LED is turned ON but no color is set. In future versions you can set a default color at the beginning.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_rgb_light_set_rgb() with the RGB LED ID with no color (all the duty cycles to 0).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_rgb_light_t.

briefdescription.para: Set the color of the RGB LED according to the intensity measured by the ultrasound sensor.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Correct the levels of the RGB LEDs according to the intensity . Use the _correct_rgb_light_levels(). ✅ 2. Call function port_rgb_light_set_rgb() with the RGB LED ID and the color ✅ 3. Reset the flag new_color to indicate that the color has been set ✅ 4. Set the RGB light system to idle. In this case, the RGB light system is active, but while the intensity is not changed, the RGB light system is idle and can enter in a low power mode

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_rgb_light_t.

briefdescription.para: Turn the RGB light system OFF.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call function port_rgb_light_set_rgb() with the RGB LED ID with no color (COLOR_OFF). ✅ 2. Reset the flag idle to indicate that the RGB light system is not idle

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_t struct than contains an fsm_rgb_light_t.

briefdescription.para: Initialize an RGB light system FSM.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the default values of the FSM struct and calls to the port to initialize the associated HW given the ID.
The FSM stores the RGB light level of the RGB light system. The user should set it using the function fsm_rgb_light_set_color_intensity().

This RGB light system is agnostic to the Simone game. It only shows the status of the RGB light system set by the user. It does not matter if the RGB light is for a game, a parking sensor, a door sensor, or any other device. The RGB light system only shows a status according to a level set by the user.
The FSM contains information of the RGB LED ID. This ID is a unique identifier that is managed by the user in the port. That is where the user provides identifiers and HW information for all the RGB LEDs on his system. The FSM does not have to know anything of the underlying HW.

simplesect.para: Both unit and integration tests (example) are provided for this ultrasound library are available in this GitHub repository: https://github.com/sdg2DieUpm/simone/tree/simone_v3_test

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call the fsm_init() to initialize the FSM. Pass the address of the fsm_t struct and the transition table. ✅ 2. Initialize the rgb_light_id. ✅ 3. Set the intensity_perc to MAX_LEVEL_INTENSITY. Initialize the color to OFF. ✅ 4. Initialize the flags new_color, status, and idle to false. ✅ 5. Call function port_rgb_light_init() to initialize the HW.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the RGB light FSM.
Unique RGB light identifier number.

briefdescription.para: Create a new RGB light FSM.

detaileddescription.para: This function creates a new RGB light FSM with the given RGB light ID.

parameterdescription.para: RGB light ID. Must be unique.

simplesect.para: fsm_rgb_light_t* Pointer to the RGB light FSM.

detaileddescription.para:
This function destroys an RGB light FSM and frees the memory.
TODO alumnos:
✅ 1. Implement this function analogously to the fsm_button_destroy() function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_rgb_light_t struct.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to fire the RGB light FSM. It is used to check the transitions and execute the actions of the RGB light FSM.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Call the fsm_fire() function. Pass the address of the fsm_t struct.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_rgb_light_t struct.

briefdescription.para: Set the color and intensity of the RGB light.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the color and intensity of the RGB light.

blockquote.para: TODO alumnos: ✅ 1. Set the color and intensity_perc fields of the RGB light system FSM. ✅ 2. Set the new_color field accordingly to indicate that a new color has to be set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_rgb_light_t struct.
RGB color to set.
Intensity in percentage to set.

briefdescription.para: Get the status of the RGB light FSM.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the status of the RGB light system. This function might be used for testing and debugging purposes.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Retrieve and return the field status.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_rgb_light_t struct.

simplesect.para: true If the RGB light system has been indicated to be active.
false If the RGB light system has been indicated to be paused.

briefdescription.para: Set the status of the RGB light FSM.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to set the status of the RGB light system. Indicating if the RGB light system is active or paused.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Update the field status with the received value

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_rgb_light_t struct.
Status of the RGB light system. true if the RGB light system is paused, false if the RGB light system is active.

briefdescription.para: Check if the RGB light system is active.

detaileddescription.para: This function checks if the RGB light system is active.

blockquote.para: ✅ 1. Return true if the RGB light system is active and it is not idle. Otherwise, return false.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an fsm_rgb_light_t struct.

simplesect.para: true If the RGB light system is active.
false If the RGB light system is inactive.

briefdescription.para: RGB light system FSM main file.
